source: A Quick Review of E-PCM for Engineering Entrance - Physics
subject: Physics

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A soap bubble (surface tension 30 × 10⁻³ N/m) has a radius 2 cm. The work done in doubling the radius is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For liquid & glass, angle of contact value is 0°. If glass tube is inserted in liquid, liquid rises up to 6 cm height. If other capillary tube has internal radius 3/2 times of previous, then liquid column height will be:
questions.explanation: hr = constant. h₁r₁ = h₂r₂. 6×r = h₂×(3r/2). h₂ = 4 cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The most characteristic property of a liquid is:
questions.explanation: Volume conservation is the most characteristic property that distinguishes liquids from gases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Soap helps in better cleaning of clothes because:
questions.optionA: It reduces the surface tension of solution
questions.explanation: Soap reduces surface tension, allowing water to spread and penetrate fabrics more easily.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two capillary tubes P and Q are dipped in water. The height of water level in capillary P is 2/3 the height in Q capillary. The ratio of their diameters is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The surface tension of soap solution is 25 × 10⁻³ N/m. The excess pressure inside a soap bubble of diameter 1 cm is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two bubbles of radii 3 cm and 5 cm coalesce to form a common surface. The radius of curvature of this common surface will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The work done in splitting a drop of water of 1 mm radius into 10⁶ droplets is (S.T. of water = 72 × 10⁻³ J/m²):

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Water rises to a height of 10 cm in a capillary tube, and mercury falls to a depth of 3.42 cm in the same capillary tube. If the density of mercury is 13.6 and the angle of contact is 135°, the ratio of surface tension for water and mercury is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A vessel, whose bottom has round holes with diameter of 0.1 mm, is filled with water. The maximum height to which the water can be filled without leakage is (S.T. of water = 75 dynes/cm, g = 1000 cm/s²):

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Water rises in a capillary tube when its one end is dipped vertically in it is 3 cm. If the surface tension of water is 75 × 10⁻³ N/m, then the diameter of capillary will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the work done in blowing a bubble of volume V is W, then the work done in blowing a soap bubble of volume 2V will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In a capillary tube water rises to a height 16 cm. The rise of water in a capillary tube of radius double to that of previous one is:
questions.explanation: h ∝ 1/r. If r doubles, h halves. h₂ = 16/2 = 8 cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: On dipping a capillary in a liquid, the liquid rises to a height of 3 cm in it. Now the capillary is cut in such a way that only a part of length l remains outside the liquid, then:
questions.optionA: Liquid will come out in form of spray
questions.optionC: Liquid will rise up to end and radius of curvature of liquid surface gets changed
questions.optionD: Liquid will rise up to end and radius of curvature of liquid surface remains unchanged
questions.explanation: If capillary length is insufficient, liquid rises to the top but meniscus radius increases (becomes less curved) to compensate.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: On dipping a capillary in a liquid inside an artificial satellite:
questions.optionA: the shape of the liquid meniscus is concave
questions.optionB: the shape of the liquid meniscus is convex
questions.optionC: the liquid will not rise at all
questions.optionD: the liquid will rise up to the length of the tube
questions.explanation: In a satellite (weightlessness), g ≈ 0, so h = 2Tcosθ/(rρg) → ∞. Water rises to the full length of the tube.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The surface tension of soap is T. The work done in blowing a soap bubble of diameter D to that of a diameter 2D is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A square frame of side L is dipped in a liquid soap. When it is taken out of the liquid, a soap film is formed on it. If the surface tension of soap is T, then the force acting on frame is:
questions.explanation: F = T × 2 × 4L = 8TL (two surfaces, 4 sides of length L each)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The work done in blowing a bubble of radius R is W, then the work done in making a bubble of radius 2R from that solution is:
